steps.title: Connect Cruz to the movement
steps.content: The text says off-off-Broadway theaters freed artists to create more experimental productions. Cruz is the example: working with those theaters let her direct her own plays exactly as she intended, even if the results seemed strange to Broadway audiences.

steps.title: Eliminate unsupported endings
steps.content: Choice A introduces production cost, which the text never discusses. Choice B says Cruz recognized off-off-Broadway staging was more complicated, which is not stated. Choice C speculates about fame and mainstream work. Cruz serves as an example of the artistic freedom offered by off-off-Broadway theaters. The answer is D.
